skills.name: Docker Patterns
skills.description: Production Dockerfile patterns: multi-stage builds, security, caching, health checks
skills.author: timps-community
skills.content:
---
name: docker-patterns
category: devops
description: Production-ready Docker patterns
---

# Docker Patterns

## Multi-Stage Node.js Dockerfile

```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Runtime stage
FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
RUN addgroup --system app && adduser --system --ingroup app app
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder --chown=app:app /app/node_modules ./node_modules
USER app
EXPOSE 3000
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
```

## .dockerignore

```
node_modules
.env
*.log
dist
.git
coverage
```

## Rules
- Always use specific tags (not `latest`)
- Run as non-root user
- Multi-stage to minimize image size
- HEALTHCHECK for production
- Never put secrets in Dockerfile or image layers

skills.name: Git Workflow
skills.description: Trunk-based development, commit conventions, PR templates, branch naming
skills.author: timps-community
skills.content:
---
name: git-workflow
category: workflow
description: Trunk-based development workflow conventions
---

# Git Workflow

## Commit Message Convention (Conventional Commits)

```
<type>(<scope>): <short summary>

[optional body]

[optional footer]
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `ci`

Examples:
```
feat(auth): add JWT refresh token rotation
fix(db): resolve N+1 query in user listing
test(api): add integration tests for /users endpoint
```

## Branch Naming

```
feat/description-of-feature
fix/short-bug-description
chore/dependency-updates
release/v1.2.0
```

## PR Template

```markdown
## What does this change?
[1-3 sentences]

## Why?
[context, links to issue]

## Testing
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] Manual testing done

## Checklist
- [ ] `tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] No console.log left in code
- [ ] Backward compatible
```

## Rules
- Main is always deployable
- Short-lived branches (< 2 days)
- Squash merge to keep main history clean
- Tag releases: `git tag v1.2.0 && git push --tags`

skills.name: Zod Validation
skills.description: Schema validation patterns with zod: parsing, transforms, custom errors, inferred types
skills.author: timps-community
skills.content:
---
name: zod-validation
category: patterns
description: Zod schema validation patterns
---

# Zod Validation

## Basic Usage

```ts
import { z } from 'zod';

const UserSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().cuid(),
  email: z.string().email(),
  age: z.number().int().min(13).max(120),
  role: z.enum(['user', 'admin', 'moderator']).default('user'),
});

type User = z.infer<typeof UserSchema>; // derive type from schema
```

## Transforms

```ts
const DateSchema = z.string().transform((s) => new Date(s));
const TrimmedString = z.string().trim().min(1);
const CoercedNumber = z.coerce.number(); // '42' -> 42
```

## Custom Errors

```ts
const PasswordSchema = z.string()
  .min(8, 'Password must be at least 8 characters')
  .regex(/[A-Z]/, 'Must contain at least one uppercase letter')
  .regex(/[0-9]/, 'Must contain at least one number');
```

## Partial / Merge for Update Schemas

```ts
const CreateUserSchema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), name: z.string() });
const UpdateUserSchema = CreateUserSchema.partial().merge(z.object({ id: z.string() }));
```

## Rules
- Always use `safeParse` (not `parse`) at API boundaries — returns `{ success, data, error }`
- Derive TypeScript types from schemas with `z.infer<typeof Schema>`
- Colocate schema with the type it validates
- Use `.describe()` on fields for better error messages and docs

skills.name: Monorepo Patterns
skills.description: npm workspaces conventions: package structure, shared configs, build order, cross-package imports
skills.author: timps-community
skills.content:
---
name: monorepo-patterns
category: architecture
description: npm workspaces monorepo patterns
---

# Monorepo Patterns

## Structure

```
root/
  package.json          # workspaces: ["packages/*", "apps/*"]
  tsconfig.base.json    # shared TS config
  packages/
    memory-core/        # @myorg/memory-core
    ui/                 # @myorg/ui
  apps/
    web/                # uses @myorg/ui
    cli/                # uses @myorg/memory-core
```

## Package Naming

```json
{ "name": "@myorg/memory-core", "version": "0.1.0" }
```

## Cross-Package Imports

```ts
// In apps/cli/package.json
{ "dependencies": { "@myorg/memory-core": "*" } }

// In apps/cli/src/index.ts
import { MemoryEngine } from '@myorg/memory-core';
```

## Shared tsconfig.base.json

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "strict": true, "target": "ES2022", "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext", "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true, "sourceMap": true
  }
}
```

## Rules
- Build shared packages before apps: `npm run build -w packages/memory-core`
- Never import from `dist/` in dev — use `exports` field with `".": { "development": "./src/index.ts" }`
- Keep circular deps at zero: `madge --circular`
- One `tsconfig.base.json` at root, extended by each package
